parameters.content:
## Cloud Cost Spike Detector & Alert
This workflow monitors daily cloud spend, compares it against a rolling baseline, and raises an alert whenever costs spike beyond an acceptable threshold. It pulls current spend from the cloud billing API, calculates the percentage increase versus the trailing average, classifies the severity, logs every run, and notifies the finance and DevOps teams before the monthly bill balloons. This prevents surprise invoices and catches runaway resources early.

### How it Works

	-	Runs automatically every morning on a schedule.
	-	Fetches the latest daily cloud cost from the billing API.
	-	Reads the historical baseline from the tracking sheet.
	-	Calculates the percentage change versus the rolling average.
	-	Filters out days where data is missing or zero.
	-	Checks whether the spike crosses the alert threshold.
	-	Classifies severity as Watch, High, or Critical.
	-	Creates an incident ticket for engineering review.
	-	Sends a detailed Slack alert and an email summary.
	-	Logs every run back to the sheet for the next baseline.

### Setup Steps

	1.	Add your cloud billing API key as an HTTP header credential.
	2.	Connect the Google Sheets account for baseline and run logging.
	3.	Set the spike threshold percentage in the Calculate node.
	4.	Connect your Slack account and select the alert channel.
	5.	Connect Gmail for the finance email summary.
	6.	Connect Jira for incident ticket creation.
	7.	Adjust the schedule time to match your billing cycle.
	8.	Activate the workflow so it runs automatically.

## Step 1: Fetch Daily Cost Data

This section runs on a daily schedule and pulls the latest cloud spend from the billing API, then loads the historical baseline from the tracking sheet. Together these provide the raw numbers needed to detect a spike.

## Step 2: Analyze & Detect Spike

These nodes clean the data, calculate the percentage change versus the rolling baseline, drop invalid rows, and decide whether the increase crosses the alert threshold. Only genuine spikes continue past this phase.

## Step 3: Alert, Ticket & Log

When a spike is confirmed, the workflow classifies the severity, opens an incident ticket for engineering, sends a Slack alert and an email summary to finance, and writes the run back to the sheet so it feeds the next baseline.

assignments.name: alertSummary
assignments.value: =Cloud cost spike on {{ $json.service }}: {{ $json.todayCost }} {{ $json.currency }} vs baseline {{ $json.baselineAvg }} {{ $json.currency }} (up {{ $json.pctChange }} percent)

parameters.summary: ={{ $json.severity }} cloud cost spike on {{ $('Calculate Spike').item.json.service }} - up {{ $('Calculate Spike').item.json.pctChange }} percent

parameters.subject: ={{ $('Classify Severity').item.json.severity }} Cloud Cost Spike - {{ $('Calculate Spike').item.json.service }}
